sections.heading: Overview
sections.paragraph: Entact Bio has strengthened its leadership in induced-proximity therapeutics by appointing John G. Houston, Ph.D., to its Board of Directors, leveraging his pivotal role in advancing the first FDA-approved PROTAC drug at Arvinas. This strategic move underscores the growing validation of proximity-inducing modalities, as Houston previously helped build Arvinas into a company that secured approval for Veppanu (vepdegestrant). Entact Bio is now applying similar induced-proximity mechanisms to develop ENTAC™ medicines, which aim to enhance the function of beneficial proteins rather than degrading harmful ones, targeting loss-of-function diseases previously out of reach for conventional therapeutics. The company’s proprietary Encompass™ platform utilizes deubiquitylase (DUB) biochemistry to regulate and enhance protein function, expanding the treatable disease universe. While broader industry updates in June 2026 included FDA pilot programs for trial acceleration and Eli Lilly’s compassionate use access for retatrutide, specific PROTAC intelligence remains anchored in these proximity-inducing advancements. Houston’s experience in taking novel mechanisms from concept to approval is deemed invaluable for advancing the ENTAC pipeline. This development highlights the maturation of protein degrader and enhancer technologies within the biopharma sector, where proximity-inducing drugs are establishing a new class of medicines that bring different proteins together to produce therapeutic effects. As the industry navigates complex regulatory landscapes, the success of PROTACs provides a foundational blueprint for next-generation precision medicines like those in Entact’s portfolio, signaling substantial opportunity for conditions underserved by current modalities.

points.text: Entact Bio — ENTAC™ — Loss-of-function disease — Appointment of John G. Houston, Ph.D., to Board of Directors to leverage PROTAC expertise.
Arvinas — Veppanu (vepdegestrant) — PROTAC — First FDA-approved proximity-inducing drug referenced as precedent.
Entact Bio: Developing precision medicines to enhance beneficial protein function via Encompass™ platform and ENTAC™ pipeline.
Arvinas: Pioneered PROTAC technology leading to FDA approval of Veppanu (vepdegestrant) for protein degradation.
ENTAC™: Induced-proximity mechanism leveraging deubiquitylase (DUB) biochemistry to enhance protein function rather than degrade.
Veppanu (vepdegestrant): PROTAC mechanism directing power toward degrading harmful proteins, recently approved by FDA.
